name: ghgrab
description: A simple, pretty terminal tool that lets you search and download files from GitHub without leaving your CLI.
readme_summary: ghgrab is a Rust-based command-line tool that provides a user-friendly terminal interface for searching and downloading specific files or entire folders from GitHub repositories without needing to perform a full `git clone`. It offers features like fuzzy search, file preview, batch downloads, and an agent mode for scripting.
key_features: Search and download files/folders from GitHub via CLI
TUI (Terminal User Interface) with fuzzy search
Agent mode for scripting with JSON output
Multiple installation methods (NPM, Cargo, pipx, Nix, AUR)
primary_use_case: To efficiently download specific assets from GitHub repositories directly from the command line, avoiding unnecessary cloning and streamlining workflows.
cover_image_prompt: A digital librarian meticulously selecting specific books from a vast, glowing library of code shelves. The librarian uses a magnifying glass that highlights desired files, and a conveyor belt smoothly transports only those selected books to a waiting delivery drone. Subtle UI elements resembling a terminal interface are visible on the librarian's tablet, showing file paths and download progress. The overall scene is clean and modern, with a focus on precision and efficiency, rendered in a vibrant 3D isometric illustration style.

name: folderhost
description: Your own private cloud in one executable. Share files, collaborate on code, and manage users without complex setup.
readme_summary: FolderHost is a self-hosted cloud platform distributed as a single, dependency-free executable. It allows users to share files, collaborate in real-time with live code editing, and manage users, offering a lightweight alternative to complex solutions like Nextcloud.
primary_use_case: To provide a simple, self-hosted cloud storage and collaboration solution that is easy to deploy and manage, especially for users seeking a lightweight alternative to more complex platforms.
cover_image_prompt: A minimalist, modern desk with a single, glowing, portable hard drive at its center. From the hard drive, streams of digital light flow outwards, transforming into organized file folders, collaborative code documents, and user icons. One stream specifically shows a code editor interface with multiple cursors, representing real-time collaboration. The background is a clean, abstract representation of a cloud. Subtle UI elements and the name 'FolderHost' are integrated into the scene. The overall mood is efficient and accessible. Style: 3D isometric illustration with a clean, bright color palette and soft lighting.

name: sentrysearch
description: Semantic search over videos using Gemini Embedding 2 or Qwen3-VL.
readme_summary: SentrySearch enables semantic search over video footage. It allows users to find specific moments within videos by typing descriptive text queries, and the system returns a trimmed clip of the relevant segment.
key_features: Uses Gemini Embedding 2 or Qwen3-VL for embeddings
Splits videos into chunks for indexing
Returns trimmed video clips as search results
Supports both API-based (Gemini) and local model backends
Configurable chunk duration, overlap, and preprocessing options
primary_use_case: Finding specific moments or events within video footage using natural language queries.
cover_image_prompt: A digital librarian meticulously sifting through a vast library of video reels, each reel representing a segment of footage. The librarian uses a glowing magnifying glass that, when focused on a reel, instantly highlights and extracts a small, relevant clip. The background shows shelves filled with video reels, and subtle UI elements displaying search queries and extracted clips float in the air. The overall mood is one of efficient discovery and clarity, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a vibrant color palette, emphasizing the ease of finding precise information within large amounts of video data.

name: awesome-seo-tools
description: Curated list of awesome SEO tools
readme_summary: This repository is a curated list of Search Engine Optimization (SEO) tools designed to help users improve their website's ranking and visibility on search engines. It categorizes various tools for different SEO needs, from keyword research to technical analysis.
key_features: Categorized tools for specific SEO needs
Includes tools for keyword research, backlink analysis, content optimization, rank tracking, technical SEO, local SEO, browser extensions, and more
Open for contributions to expand the list
primary_use_case: Discovering and selecting SEO tools to enhance website performance and search engine rankings.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian in a vast, organized library, meticulously sorting and cataloging glowing books. Each book represents an SEO tool, and the librarian is placing them onto shelves labeled with categories like 'Keyword Research,' 'Backlink Analysis,' and 'Technical SEO.' As books are placed, faint streams of data flow from them, illuminating the library and creating a subtle glow around the librarian. Small, holographic screens float nearby, displaying simple charts and website traffic indicators. The repository name 'Awesome SEO Tools' is subtly etched on the librarian's desk. The scene is rendered in a warm, inviting 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing clarity and organization.

name: winscript
description: Open-source tool to build your Windows script from scratch. It includes debloat, privacy, performance & app installing scripts.
readme_summary: WinScript is an open-source Windows customization tool that allows users to debloat unnecessary applications, enhance privacy by disabling data collection, optimize performance by fine-tuning system settings, and easily install desired software using package managers like Chocolatey or Winget.
key_features: Debloat pre-installed applications and Windows features
Enhance privacy by disabling telemetry and data collection
Optimize system performance through various tweaks
Bulk install applications using Chocolatey or Winget
primary_use_case: Customizing and optimizing a Windows installation for improved privacy, performance, and a cleaner user experience.
cover_image_prompt: A digital gardener meticulously pruning a lush, vibrant Windows operating system tree. The gardener is using glowing digital shears to remove 'bloatware' branches, represented by dark, tangled vines, while carefully nurturing the 'performance' and 'privacy' leaves, which are depicted as bright, healthy foliage. Small icons representing apps like browsers and utilities float gently, ready to be planted. Subtle UI elements and code snippets are integrated into the background, hinting at the underlying script. The scene is set in a clean, modern digital landscape with a soft, inviting glow, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with clear, distinct elements.
